prevalence.value: <0.1% (mostly travel-associated; higher in some migrant groups)
prevalence.source: ECDC/WHO travel and enteric infection context; parasitology surveillance syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; cases concentrated in travelers and imported infections

incidence.source: ECDC/WHO Europe infectious disease surveillance context

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low overall; severe invasive disease rare but clinically important
burden.mortalityRate: Very low with timely diagnosis and treatment

riskFactors: crowded settings with poor sanitation (rare in EU)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Stool antigen/PCR preferred; microscopy less specific; evaluate amoebic liver abscess with imaging and serology when clinically indicated

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Low thousands of cases across the EU annually (mostly imported; uncertain)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with timely treatment; public health control focuses on travel hygiene

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Modern diagnostics (PCR/antigen) available in many labs; travel history emphasized
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Metronidazole/tinidazole for invasive disease plus luminal agent; supportive care; travel and hygiene counseling
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Recognition in primary care when travel history is missed; differentiating from other causes of diarrhea
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Good diagnostic and treatment availability and low baseline transmission
